name: TextureEvaluator
summary: This is the interface to a lightweight object capable of evaluating texture color throughout uvw space. Derive from this class to create your own texture evaluator to return from a custom RenderTexture.

params.name: disposing
params.doc: True to release both managed and unmanaged resources; false to release only unmanaged resources

methods.name: GetColor(Point3d, Vector3d, Vector3d)
methods.signature: public virtual Color4f GetColor(Point3d uvw, Vector3d duvwdx, Vector3d duvwdy)
methods.summary: Get the color of the texture at a particular point in uvw space. May be called from within a rendering shade pipeline. note For ray differentials see Pharr Humphreys, "Physically Based Rendering", chapter 11.

params.name: uvw
params.doc: is the point for which to evaluate the texture.

returns.doc: The texture color at this point in UV space.

methods.name: GetColor(Point3d, Vector3d, Vector3d, Color4f)
methods.signature: public bool GetColor(Point3d uvw, Vector3d duvwdx, Vector3d duvwdy, ref Color4f color)
methods.summary: Optimized version of GetColor for callers. Much faster in the case of a native (C++) evaluator.

params.name: uvw
params.doc: is the point for which to evaluate the texture.

params.name: color
params.doc: The texture color at this point in UV space.

returns.doc: True if the evaluator returned a valid color. Otherwise false.

methods.name: Initialize
methods.summary: Call this function before calling GetColor for the first time. Ideally, this should be on the main thread, but you can also call it on a worker thread as long as you are sure that Initialize() or GetColor() cannot be called at the same time on another thread.

methods.name: WriteToByteArray
methods.signature: [ObsoleteAttribute("Use WriteToByteArray2 instead - supports much larger byte arrays")] public virtual SimpleArrayByte WriteToByteArray(int width, int height)
methods.summary: Fast access to bitmap evaluator - supply size (which you will probably have received from CRhRdkTexture::PixelSize) to see if the data can be extracted direct to a width*height*4 array of unsigned chars.

params.name: width
params.doc: is the point for which to evaluate the texture.

returns.doc: A ByteArray full of the byte values in RGBA order, or null if the function did not succeed

methods.name: WriteToByteArray2
methods.signature: public virtual StdVectorByte WriteToByteArray2(int width, int height)
methods.summary: Fast access to bitmap evaluator - supply size (which you will probably have received from CRhRdkTexture::PixelSize) to see if the data can be extracted direct to a width*height*4 array of unsigned chars.

params.name: width
params.doc: is the point for which to evaluate the texture.

returns.doc: A ByteArray full of the byte values in RGBA order, or null if the function did not succeed

methods.name: WriteToFloatArray
methods.signature: [ObsoleteAttribute("Use WriteToFloatArray2 instead - supports much larger byte arrays")] public virtual SimpleArrayFloat WriteToFloatArray(int width, int height)
methods.summary: Fast access to bitmap evaluator - supply size (which you will probably have received from CRhRdkTexture::PixelSize) to see if the data can be extracted direct to a width*height*4 array of unsigned chars.

params.name: width
params.doc: is the point for which to evaluate the texture.

returns.doc: A FloatArray full of the float values in RGBA order, or null if the function did not succeed.

methods.name: WriteToFloatArray2
methods.signature: public virtual StdVectorFloat WriteToFloatArray2(int width, int height)
methods.summary: Fast access to bitmap evaluator - supply size (which you will probably have received from CRhRdkTexture::PixelSize) to see if the data can be extracted direct to a width*height*4 array of unsigned chars.

params.name: width
params.doc: is the point for which to evaluate the texture.

returns.doc: A FloatArray full of the float values in RGBA order, or null if the function did not succeed.
